meta.caption:
Most pillows are designed for back sleepers. 74% of us sleep on our sides.

Standard pillows put your spine at a 30 degree angle when you sleep on your side. That's why you wake up feeling like someone twisted your neck while you were out.

A side sleeper pillow keeps your spine straight. Eli & Elm uses a U-shape with a shoulder cutout and adjustable latex-fiber fill so you can dial in the height for your shoulder width.

Spine stays straight all night. Wake up the same way you fell asleep — relaxed, not crooked.

Comment PILLOW and I'll DM you the link.
Affiliate link — I earn a small commission at no extra cost to you.

#amazonhomefinds #sleepbetter #sidesleeper #bedroomupgrade

meta.caption:
Picture this: it is 4am, your neck won't turn left, and the alarm is in 90 minutes.

You stare at the ceiling. Your shoulder is numb. The pillow under your head is somehow both too high and too low. You flip it. Cold side, sure, but the angle is still wrong.

An adjustable side sleeper pillow with a shoulder cutout fixes the angle problem. Eli & Elm — latex-fiber fill you can add or remove, U-shape that lets your shoulder rest naturally.

Same bed, same room, same body. The only thing that changed was the pillow. The 4am stare-at-the-ceiling routine just stopped.

meta.caption:
I spent ten years thinking my mattress was the problem.

I bought a $1,400 mattress to fix my neck pain. Didn't help. Bought a topper. Didn't help. Saw a chiropractor twice a month for two years. Helped, but the pain came back every time.

Turns out it was the pillow. Specifically the wrong pillow for a side sleeper. The Eli & Elm has a shoulder cutout that lets your shoulder drop into the bed instead of crushing it.

Three weeks of using it and the chronic stiffness is just gone. Saved myself another mattress.

meta.caption:
The reason your cabinets stay messy is that nothing has a home.

Containers don't fix this. Bigger cabinets don't fix this. The problem is depth — when you can't reach the back without removing the front, the back becomes invisible.

A sliding basket organizer fixes the depth problem. Simple Houseware's chrome 2-tier model holds 18 lbs per shelf — each tier pulls forward independently, so the back row is always one motion away.

Stopped buying duplicates of stuff I already owned. The cabinet became a system instead of a graveyard.
